ProjectBlueprint.description: A mobile application that leverages smartphone camera technology and AI-powered image recognition to diagnose diseases, pests, and health issues in houseplants, providing users with actionable care recommendations.

productRequirementsDocument.overview: PlantDoc is a plant-care companion app that uses computer vision and machine learning to identify houseplant species, diagnose health issues, and provide personalized care recommendations through the user's smartphone camera.
objectives: Enable users to diagnose plant health issues via camera in under 10 seconds
Achieve 85% accuracy in disease and pest identification
Build a personalized plant care library for each user
Provide actionable treatment plans for diagnosed issues
productRequirementsDocument.targetAudience: Houseplant owners ranging from beginners to enthusiasts who want quick, reliable plant health diagnostics without consulting experts.
productRequirementsDocument.scope: Mobile application (iOS and Android) with camera-based plant diagnostics, care recommendations, plant library, and reminder system.

epics.title: Camera-Based Plant Diagnosis
epics.description: Core functionality allowing users to photograph plants and receive AI-powered health diagnostics.

epics.title: Plant Care Library
epics.description: Personal library where users track their plants and view care history.

epics.title: Care Recommendations & Reminders
epics.description: Personalized treatment plans and scheduled care reminders for user plants.

epics.title: User Account Management
epics.description: Account creation, authentication, and profile management.

userStories.title: Photograph plant for diagnosis
userStories.iWant: to take a photo of my sick plant using the app
userStories.soThat: I can quickly identify what is wrong with it
acceptanceCriteria: User can access camera from home screen in one tap
App provides framing guides for optimal photo capture
User can retake photo before submission

userStories.title: Receive diagnosis results
userStories.iWant: to see a clear diagnosis of my plant's condition
userStories.soThat: I understand what is affecting my plant
acceptanceCriteria: Multiple possible conditions shown if applicable
Visual examples of the identified condition provided

userStories.title: Save plants to library
userStories.iWant: to save my plants to a personal library
userStories.soThat: I can track their health over time
acceptanceCriteria: User can name and categorize each plant
Diagnosis history is saved per plant
Plants can be edited or deleted

userStories.title: Get treatment recommendations
userStories.soThat: I can nurse my plant back to health

userStories.title: Set care reminders
userStories.iWant: to receive watering and care reminders
userStories.soThat: I do not forget to maintain my plants

userStories.title: Create user account
userStories.soThat: my plant data syncs across devices
acceptanceCriteria: Email and social login options available

gherkinScenarios.given: the user has opened the PlantDoc app and granted camera permissions
gherkinScenarios.when: the user taps the camera button and takes a photo of their plant
gherkinScenarios.then: the app displays the captured image with options to retake or submit for diagnosis

gherkinScenarios.given: the user has submitted a clear photo of a diseased plant
gherkinScenarios.when: the AI processing completes within 10 seconds
gherkinScenarios.then: the app displays the diagnosis with confidence score, severity level, and visual reference images

gherkinScenarios.scenario: User saves a new plant to library
gherkinScenarios.given: the user has completed a diagnosis scan
gherkinScenarios.when: the user taps Save to Library and enters plant details
gherkinScenarios.then: the plant appears in the user's library with diagnosis history attached

gherkinScenarios.given: the user has received a plant diagnosis
gherkinScenarios.when: the user taps View Treatment Plan
gherkinScenarios.then: the app displays step-by-step instructions, required materials, and recovery timeline

gherkinScenarios.given: the user has set a watering reminder for a plant
gherkinScenarios.then: a push notification is delivered with options to mark complete or snooze

tasks.title: Implement camera integration module
tasks.description: Build native camera interface with framing guides and photo capture functionality for iOS and Android.

tasks.title: Build image preprocessing pipeline
tasks.description: Implement image compression, cropping, and optimization before ML inference.

tasks.title: Integrate ML diagnosis model
tasks.description: Connect plant disease classification model via API or on-device inference.

tasks.title: Design diagnosis results UI
tasks.description: Create UI screens showing diagnosis, confidence, severity, and reference imagery.

tasks.title: Build plant library database
tasks.description: Design and implement local and cloud data storage for user plants and history.

tasks.title: Create treatment content system
tasks.description: Build CMS and data models for treatment plans tied to diagnoses.

tasks.title: Implement push notification system
tasks.description: Set up push notifications and reminder scheduling engine.

tasks.title: Build authentication system
tasks.description: Implement email and social authentication with secure token management.

priorities.rationale: Core camera functionality is essential to the product value proposition and must work flawlessly at launch.
Diagnosis accuracy is the primary difAferentiator and user expectation; critical for MVP.
Treatment recommendations deliver the actionable value that transforms diagnosis into outcomes.
Plant library enhances retention but is not required for initial diagnosis value delivery.
Reminders drive engagement but can be released in a post-MVP iteration.
Account creation supports data sync but guest mode allows MVP usage without accounts.
